what_it_does: A research finding that traces how loadouts/combos currently declare only a set of service keys, argues that per-service shape-intent is missing, refutes alternative resolutions, and proposes a design to attach computed shape-intent to combos and thread it through the switch/gate/actuator spine.
observations: A combo today resolves to only a list of service keys plus variant grammar; there is no per-service config layer inside the combo.
All serve shape (gpu_util, max_model_len, max_num_seqs, capability_overrides, _profile) lives on the single config block of each service key in services.json.
Two loadouts of the same model with different computed budgets is currently impossible because the same service key reads the identical config block.

Option (b) one instance serving both conversational and worker shapes via priority/chunked-prefill is refuted on two grounds: opposite co-residency requirements and vLLM init-time max_num_seqs/KV-pool allocation.
Sleep-mode is a time-share between already-loaded instances at fixed shapes, not a shape reshaper; its proper home is the model-swap axis and it is flagged broken on some models.
Serve-flag toggles (priority, kv_offload, sleep, enforce_eager) are proposed to become capability-type rows with serve_values, mirroring the existing kv_cache_dtype pattern.
Sizing shape-intent (max_num_seqs, max_model_len, kv budget) is argued to need a new per-service shape block because it feeds both serve flags and gpu.py budget math.
A blocking sub-gap is identified: ensure_resident returns early when a service is already resident, so a declared shape change would be silently ignored.
_resolve_loadout and _gate_work_requires are presence-only today, creating a risk that work requiring a worker loadout passes the gate while the brain is resident in the conversational shape.
chat-4b-fp8 currently has _profile: null, so computed budgets for this brain are not yet live and depend on a sibling profiler area.
standouts: The real architectural gap is not budget computation but the absence of a per-service shape carrier on the combo.
The decisive refutation of one-instance-serves-both-shapes is opposite co-residency, not reload cost.
ensure_resident's already-resident no-op is a silent-degrade blocker that must be fixed for any shape-overlay to work.
Shape-intent straddles two homes: capability rows for serve-flag toggles, and a new per-service shape block for sizing inputs to budget math.
conventions: Uses explicit evidentiary markers: Observed, Inferred, External, My-idea.
Cites specific file:line ranges for claims.
Numbered sections with tables summarizing mappings and design options.
Includes a dedicated contradictions/corrections section addressing the anchor document.
Ends with open threads and honest caveats rather than claiming all sub-problems are solved.
concerns: The proposed shape carrier is left as either a free-form combo overlay or a typed loadout_shape registry, with a preference but no decision.
Computed budgets for chat-4b-fp8 depend on _profile data that is currently null, so the mechanism cannot be validated end-to-end yet.
Claims about vLLM init-time max_num_seqs behavior rely on external issues and a prior research artifact, not re-verified this pass.
The shape-match enforcement requires reading the live resident shape, and the mechanism for doing so is unverified.
notes: A research finding arguing that loadouts need a per-service shape-intent layer on combos, tracing why one instance cannot serve both conversational and worker shapes, and naming the ensure_resident already-resident no-op as a silent-degrade blocker.
questions: How will the system read the live resident max_num_seqs for shape-match checks: via a vLLM endpoint or recorded session state?
Will the shape layer be a free-form JSON overlay on each combo or a typed, reusable loadout_shape registry?
Does an absolute KV byte budget truly remove load-order sensitivity for non-vLLM co-tenants such as embed-pplx and kokoro?
What is the verified status of sleep-mode on chat-4b-fp8, given it is flagged broken since vLLM 0.14?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to add an interpretive, neutral, file-local ledger layer for a code/design substrate; the file is a research finding analyzing loadout declaration and attachment, which matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: No silent degrade: shape must be matched at both the gate and the actuator.
Separation of the set grammar (which services are resident) from the shape grammar (how a service is sized).
Computed budgets replacing hand-typed config values.
Per-loadout resource intent flowing through the existing switch+gate+actuator spine.
intention_signals: It is trying to dissolve hand-edited service config shapes by making loadouts declare shape-intent, compute budgets from that intent, and thread shape through apply_loadout, ensure_resident, serveconfig, and gpu.py so the same model can have distinct conversational and worker loadouts without duplication or silent mismatches.
novelty: It explicitly reframes the anchor's question by identifying the missing per-service shape carrier on combos as the primary gap rather than budget computation, and it locates a concrete blocking bug in ensure_resident's already-resident short-circuit.
contribution: A detailed, evidence-traced argument that the combo needs a per-service shape layer; a concrete wiring chain showing where shape must thread; a refutation of one-instance-serves-both-shapes grounded in co-residency and init-time constraints; and a named silent-degrade blocker in ensure_resident that any fused design must address.
summary_for_embedding: Research finding on AREA-3 of the gpu-serving rework that analyzes how loadouts/combos currently carry only service keys, identifies the missing per-service shape-intent carrier, refutes one-instance-serves-both-shapes, and proposes threading computed shape through apply_loadout, ensure_resident, serveconfig, and gpu.py budget math.
intention_for_embedding: To establish that the loadout layer must declare per-service shape-intent so the same model can have distinct conversational and worker loadouts without hand-edited configs or silent shape mismatches.
